revision.text:
[[ja:乾漆棺]] [[File:AsukaTenjishitu Kengoshizuka06.jpg|thumb|200px|The lacquered coffin excavated from {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|qid=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun|1=Kengoshizuka Kofun}} (on display at the Asuka Village Archaeological Display Room)]] {{nihongo|'''Dry-lacquer coffins'''|乾漆棺|Kanshitsukan}} are a type of coffin crafted using the {{ill|Kyōcho|ja|夾紵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōcho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} technique, which involves layering {{ill|hemp cloth|ja|麻織物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp cloth|qq=}} with lacquer. It is also referred to as a '''Kyōcho coffin''' (夾紵棺). There are two types of lacquered coffins: hollow-core kanshitsukan, made entirely of multiple layers of hemp cloth, and wooden-core kanshitsukan, in which a wooden coffin is reinforced with cloth and lacquer. Lacquered coffins are found in a select few {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tombs and are considered to have been used for the burial of elites during that era. == Kofun with dry-lacquer coffins == Examples of kanshitsukan are extremely rare. All known examples are associated with members of the {{ill|Imperial family (Japan)|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial family (Japan)|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} or other high-Ranking individuals. * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} ({{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|qid=Q860659|lt=Gyōda|1=Gyōda}}, Saitama Prefecture, possibly {{ill|Mononobe no Arimaro|ja|物部連兄麿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Arimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Marukoyama Kofun|en|Marukoyama Kofun|qid=Q11341265|lt=Marukoyama Kofun|1=Marukoyama Kofun}} ({{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, possibly {{ill|Prince Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kawashima|qq=}}) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|qid=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun|1=Kengoshizuka Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara Prefecture, possibly {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}) * {{ill|Kosotsukamikado Kofun|lt=Kosotsukamikado Kofun|lt=Etsuzuka Mikado Kofun|ja|越塚御門古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Etsuzuka Mikado Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara Prefecture, possibly {{ill|Princess Ōta|en|Princess Ōta|qid=Q603057|lt=Princess Ōta|1=Princess Ōta}}) * {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Mausoleum|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Mausoleum|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara Prefecture, burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|qid=Q1039675|lt=Takamikura Kofun|1=Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} (Asuka, Nara Prefecture, possibly a son of Emperor Tenmu) * {{ill|Abuyama Kofun|en|Abuyama Kofun|qid=Q11657465|lt=Abuyama Kofun|1=Abuyama Kofun}} ({{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki, Osaka|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture, possibly {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|qid=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}) * {{ill|Eifukuji Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|qid=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture, possibly {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} or {{ill|Kashiwade no Oiratsume|ja|膳部菩岐々美郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Oiratsume|qq=}}) In addition, a fragment of a kyōcho coffin is preserved at {{ill|Anpukuji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpukuji (Kashiwara)|1=Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451150}} in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara|1=Kashiwara}}, Osaka Prefecture. Since there are no late Kofun-period tombs near Anpukuji, the fragment is thought to have come from Eifukuji Kofun and has been designated as a Kashiwara City Cultural Property.{{Cite web|title=7-4.夾紵棺 fragment preserved at Anpukuji |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016110100057/?doc_id=5647 |website=Kashiwara City Official Website |access-date=2023-06-25}}. File:安福寺所蔵 夾紵棺片.JPG|Kyōcho coffin fragment preserved at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Kashiwara City Historical Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Hajime Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Ōtsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433969}}, {{ill|Mitsunori Tozawa|ja|戸沢充則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsunori Tozawa|1=Tozawa Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11075419}} (editors), *Latest Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms*, Shinso-sha, June 1996, p. 74. * {{ill|Koichi Tamura|ja|田村晃一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Tamura|1=Kōichi Tamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17161065}}, {{ill|Yoshimasa Gōda|ja|合田芳正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimasa Gōda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (editors), *Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration*, Yamakawa Publishing, July 2000, p. 27. == See Also == * {{ill|Dry-lacquer sculpture|ja|乾漆造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer sculpture|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乾漆棺|version=100336810|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乾漆棺]] {{wikidata link|Q17990284}} [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
